Farm Log V1 — Ranch Tracker

Cattle, feed, land, maintenance, media, finance, and reporting tracker with ranch-specific charts and import/export tools.

Images are stored in the tracker state so your exported JSON/YML and PDF can carry cattle profiles, feed and hay visuals, and maintenance photos. Large image-heavy ranch books are saved to IndexedDB instead of relying on small localStorage quotas.

Ranch Tracker

Track cattle nicknames, sickness, pregnancy, births, feeding, hay/feed, sales, acquisitions, land, pens, fencing, and photos with export-ready reporting.

Ranch alerts

Low inventory, due cows, open health cases, and overdue maintenance.

Operational snapshot

Quick KPIs for data-driven ranching decisions.

Herd status

Active, sold, deceased, held

Pregnancy pipeline

Open, exposed, pregnant, postpartum

Health incidents

Monthly count of logged cattle issues

Feed remaining

Current on-hand hay, feed, supplements

Feed usage trend

Drawdown logged over time

Financial flow

Sales, acquisitions, and expenses

Maintenance cost by asset type

Land, pens, fences, wire, and more

Add or update cattle profile

Nickname cattle, assign pens and pastures, attach photos, and track lifecycle data.

Selected cattle profile

Full profile, images, and event timeline.

Select a cattle card below to inspect its full record.

Herd roster

Cards carry images, nickname, pregnancy, pen, and lifecycle status.

Log cattle health event

Track sickness, symptoms, vitals, treatment, follow-up, and photos.

Cattle doctor / triage assistant

Rule-based cattle issue triage. It is a recordkeeping aid, not a veterinarian.

Run an assessment to rank likely issue buckets, urgency, and documentation prompts.

Health logbook

Every issue is timestamped and linked to the animal profile.

Saved doctor assessments

Record of cattle-health triage runs with urgency.

Feed / hay inventory

Remaining feed, reorder points, lot locations, and photos.

Feed usage log

Subtract inventory, track burn-down, destination, and herd group.

Inventory cards

Includes remaining quantity and estimated days on hand.

Feed usage logbook

Every feed draw event supports inventory and charting.

Breeding / pregnancy event

Track heats, bred dates, preg checks, and due dates.

Birth / calving record

Create a timestamped birth event with dam, calf, and photo evidence.

Breeding log

Timestamped reproductive events for herd and reports.

Birth log

Calving records with dam, calf linkage, and media.

Sales / acquisitions / expenses

Track cattle purchases, cattle sales, hay/feed spend, and ranch costs.

Finance snapshot

Current totals and trend highlights.

Transaction register

Every sale, acquisition, and expense is reportable.

Land / pen / fence / asset record

Track land, pens, fencing, barbed wire, troughs, barns, alleys, and equipment.

Maintenance event

Timestamp repair, service, material cost, labor, and visuals.

Asset register

Land, pens, water, wire runs, and facilities with photos.

Maintenance logbook

Overdue and completed work remains visible for charts and reports.

Add media record

Standalone photos for cattle, hay, feed, fencing, barbed wire, land, and notes.

Media vault summary

Photos are included in state exports and profile/report rendering.

Gallery

Aggregate images across cattle, inventory, assets, maintenance, and standalone media.

Export ranch book

JSON, CSV, YML, and PDF with timestamps, charts, and visuals.

CSV export generates separate collection files and zips them when JSZip is available. JSON and YML exports include images and nested records intact.

Import ranch book

Replace or merge data from JSON, YML, and CSV collections.

CSV import expects separate files such as cattle.csv, health_logs.csv, feed_inventory.csv, transactions.csv, and so on. Imported collections replace matching sections of the current state.

Report readiness

Snapshot of what will be included in the next export.

Collection sizes

Helps verify complete ranch data coverage.

Searchable ranch field guide

Built-in operational reference, data dictionary, playbooks, and cattle issue guidance.

Selected article

Operational guidance and recordkeeping reference stored in this file.

Select an article or playbook from the list to read it here.
The field guide includes ranch operations reference material, data dictionary entries, maintenance playbooks, and cattle-health documentation prompts alongside the live tracker.
